observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin all offices concerned particularly the treasury office and budget office to submit the needed data and reports to accounting on time and observe cut-off of transactions. Preparation of FS is a concerted effort and everyone is enjoined for the timely submission of FS.



We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require concerned Municipal Department Heads to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the disclosure of financial and non-financial information (e.g.  key management personnel, purchase/disposal of PPEs, etc.) to enable the latter to make necessary disclosures of relevant information in the NFSs as provided in the IPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant implemented cut-off of transactions for timely recording and submission of the financial statement.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:

The Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records pertaining to Land account. Determine the acquisition cost of each land parcel composing the Land account; and

The Municipal Assessor to exert effort to document the legality of ownership for the 20 parcels of lot through a TCT in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Recompute the proper depreciation expense for the year based on the revised depreciation schedule and reflect the adjustments as prior period adjustment;



Review the accounting policies applied and estimates made by the Municipality on its PPE and determine whether they a) conform with IPSAS 17 or b) are consistently and uniformly applied on all PPE items or class. Updates must be undertaken if needed; and

Complete and update the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each item and class of PPE and prepare depreciation schedule by including relevant data on disposal, accumulated depreciation of disposed properties, salvage values, and unrecorded acquisitions from the previous years, if there are, based on the updated PPELC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started in identifying the PPEs subject to depreciation in preparation for the lapsing schedule of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:

That the MDRRMO facilitate attendance to obtain technical training on the preparation and formulation of PSCP;



Thereafter, formulate and prepare the PSCP and to submit the report to the Municipal Mayor for approval and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP; and

Adopt a continuous monitoring of operational requirements and updating of the plan to adopt with the ever-changing environmental and related conditions.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:
To create an Inventory Committee composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency.
To instruct the Property Supply Officer on updating and renewing the PARs and AREs issued to end-users, and to identify properties issued to retired or resigned employees or officials that were not properly turned over, while also updating the RPCPPE through conducting a physical inventory of assets;
For the Municipal Accountant:
To provide updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) to the Inventory Committee, including all newly acquired assets;
After the conclusion of activities for One-Time Cleansing, provide the accounting entries to effect the result of the One-Time Cleansing
Provide sufficient disclosures on PPE in the Notes to FS on the total amount of non-existent/ missing PPEs included in the PPE balance;
For the Inventory Committee members:
Develop a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) with assigned duties, cut-off dates, and a detailed schedule of inventory activities for approval by the Municipal Mayor.
Base the PIP on the latest RPCPPE, Inventory Count Form (Annex A), and PPELCs, in coordination with the Property and Accounting Units.
If no RPCPPE exists, use the ICF format (Annex A) and instruct all offices to submit lists of assigned PPEs; ensure inventory is conducted per the approved PIP, indicating each item’s condition.
For missing, unrecorded, or non-existent PPEs, follow COA Circular 2020-006 procedures.
With the Supply Officer’s assistance, accomplish Annexes A, B, and C of the same Circular.
Lastly, Disposal Committee shall be created pursuant to COA-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2024-1, dated January 30, 2024. This committee shall effect the disposal of PPEs as a result of the One-Time Cleansing of accounts.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Take up the necessary adjusting/ correcting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality to fairly present the advance payments of RPT and SET collected in CY 2024 to wit:
Adhere to the correct accounting procedures for RPT and SET collections to accurately reflect the amounts of RPT and SET income, receivables, and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
Municipal Assessor to resume providing the list of assessed real properties to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office;
Municipal Treasurer to use the list provided by the Municipal Assessor in updating the RPTAR/TIC, which will be used as the basis in the preparation of the revised list of RPT receivables, and to submit certified list of taxpayers’ names and the amount due and collectible for each year to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant to make a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to set-up an accurate RPT Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance to Section 20 of MNGAS which is necessary in order to fairly present the actual financial position of the Municipal Government.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor  to take steps to transfer the buildings by formally requesting the SB to pass a resolution that authorizes the transfer of these assets to the designated recipient local government units and national agencies as mandated under Sec. 22 (c) RA No. 7160.
Furthermore, upon securing the SB resolution and necessary donation documents, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the appropriate adjusting entry to formally recognize the donation. To prevent an artificial inflation of expenses in the current year – which could distort financial performance and potentially result in an operating loss – the adjustment should not be recorded as a current-year expense. Instead, since these assets pertain to prior periods, they should have been classified as donations in previous years. We suggest closing the accounts under Prior Period Adjustments. The recommended adjusting entry is as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment					xxx
Accumulated Depreciation					xxx
Buildings 						xxx
School Buildings						xxx
Other Structures						xxx
Other Land Improvements					xxx
Lastly, to ensure comparability and consistency in financial reporting, the Municipal Accountant should prepare restated financial statements alongside the annual financial statements. These restated statements should accurately reflect the impact of the depreciation expenses recognized in previous years. This will enhance the reliability and transparency of financial information, enabling stakeholders to make well-informed comparisons across reporting periods and support financial decision-making.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the concerned Department Heads, to secure and submit the lacking documentary requirements necessary to support the payments made for the purchased lots to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Presidential Decree No. 1445.
Furthermore, we have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant—the official responsible for certifying the completeness of supporting documents in disbursement vouchers—to ensure that all claims are fully substantiated with the required documentation prior to the release of any payment.
Finally, Management is encouraged to review and strengthen existing internal control procedures to ensure full compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing the disbursement of public funds. This will safeguard the integrity of financial transactions and promote accountability.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the management to:
Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis in accordance with the procedures and requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5 of said COA Circular;
Ensure timely and proper planning and programming of disaster-related projects/activities to facilitate their implementation that will benefit the constituents of the Municipality; and
Ensure full adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person to undertake the following measures:
Conduct a thorough evaluation of the proposed or attributed PPAs to ensure they are genuinely responsive to identified gender-related issues and concerns;
Provide additional supporting documentation or justification to substantiate the mainstreamed PPAs; and
Regularly update the GAD Database to facilitate the accurate identification, monitoring, and analysis of gender issues.
These actions will enhance the integrity and effectiveness of the municipality's GAD initiatives, ensure proper classification of PPAs, and align with relevant policies and guidelines.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct:

●	The Engineering Office/Implementing Office to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the SWAs of the projects as of December 31, 2023;



●	The Municipal Accountant to recognize the costs of the projects equivalent to percentage of actual accomplishment reported in the SWA and RGPPA as Accounts payable and PPE/CIP in the books; and

●	The Municipal Accountant to timely record all projects based on percentage of completion as inspected and declared by the Implementing Office for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:

To hire or assign personnel as General Services Officer (GSO), considering the vacancy of the item, to supervise the monitoring of properties and maintenance of property records of the Municipality,


Create an Inventory Committee that has adequate personnel and at least one representative from the Accounting Department and the Supplies Department of the LGU who will conduct annual physical count of PPE and subsequently prepare the RPCPPE which shall be submitted to the Auditor’s Office not later than January 31 of each year, and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the following:

The Municipal Accountant to record purchased inventory on their appropriate asset account, use perpetual inventory method and for subsequent valuation, use weighted average method. Also, the MA should maintain a Supply Ledger Card for each commodity/stock. 

The Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of GSO, likewise maintain stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per stock card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit. 

Per Department Offices to use the RIS to record used inventory. This shall serve as monitoring of inventories requested and issued.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer to coordinate with the Department Offices in the consolidation of RIS and to prepare a weekly SSMI.

Additionally, for the Municipal Accountant to use the SSMI as a basis of recording Inventories expense.

Lastly, to require the Inventory Committee to conduct a periodic physical inventory of supplies or property. The physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). This shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant started to record inventories using perpetual inventory record. Further, she maintained Supplies Ledger Card for record purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the concerned municipal employee to:

Reconcile the Due to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PHIC accounts with the actual amounts withheld to identify the under and over-remittances incurred from prior years until the current year to correct balances of each account.

The reverted funds shall be available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting and utilization of cash advance. The Municipal Accountant shall limit granting of cash advance to allowable transactions stated therein to avoid any risk of possible misapplication or misuse of local government funds. Granting of cash advances for the purpose of paying suppliers and creditor thru cash, deprived the LGU of the control/ convenience/ or security of the local government funds which check payments could provide against loss, theft, misuse and misapplication.
observations.managementAction: The municipal Accountant limits the granting of cash advance to allowable transactions stated therein to avoid any risk of possible misapplication or misuse of local government funds.

observations.managementAction: Creation of General Service Officer position has been approved and given top priority by the LCE. The Sangguniang Bayan has passed the first reading on the creation of office and officer of the proposed position and its corresponding budgetary requirements. Tentative publication is targeted to be done within the current year 2022.

The management did not take any actions, as the new Budget Officer assumed their position after the completion of the annual budget. They have committed to implement the recommendations in the budget for the year 2025.

No actions were taken by the management due to insufficient manpower to handle the inventory of supplies. Additionally, a General Services Office (GSO) will be established next year to manage the inventory system and implement internal controls.

The management has ensured that the Municipal Treasurer reconciles and deposits daily collections intact into the correct fund accounts, with immediate transfers for check deposits when needed. Additionally, the cashbooks and ledgers are regularly updated to reflect accurate collections and deposits.

Demand letters were sent to Punong Barangays which have jurisdiction over the concerned individuals thru a registered mail which were duly received by the PB’s. 

The MSWDO is in close coordination with DSWD FO –I on next steps to be taken after sending the demand letters.
The Management submitted the AAPSI to the relevant government agencies and uploaded it to their website.

The LCE together with the members of the LSB strictly and promptly implemented all PPAs under SEF.

Management conducted monthly meeting to address the priority needs of public schools and assessed all PPAs appropriated for proper disposition and utilization of funds.
The Management attended the BLGF-RO I seminar on the updating of the Local Revenue Code on April 16-19, 2024. Ongoing preparation of initial draft of the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.
The Sangguniang Bayan no longer grant or extend Financial Assistances (FAs) relative to sports equipment.
Tagging of donated properties before release was being implemented.

Strengthen its control mechanism to reconcile the records pertaining to the over-remittance and under-remittance of Inter-Agency Payables.

Regularly comply with the law particularly on the remittance of statutory liabilities to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors.

observations.recommendation:
That Management ensure that the payment of honorarium of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat must be sourced only from funds specifically authorized and allowed under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

That Management must first exhaust the collections from the sale of bidding documents in payment of BAC honoraria and in case of deficiency, use the agency savings with prior approval of the local Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following measures to reflect the correct cash in bank balances:

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to request from the depository bank, copies of bank statements in order to verify and determine the nature of those debit memoranda as a basis for the adjustments to arrive at correct cash in bank balance.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the checks issued to avoid accumulation of stale checks. Required adjusting journal entry/ies be prepared immediately for stale checks which are still in the hands of the payee by recognizing accounts payable with corresponding debit to cash in bank. Moreover, replacement of stale checks be done only when the original check is presented for payment.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant to conduct verification of the unbooked bank deposit before preparing adjusting journal entry in order that reconciling items no longer appear in the succeeding bank reconciliation statements.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expense, prepare the lapsing schedule and draw necessary adjusting journal entry to take up depreciation expense for the current year.

We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant update the related accumulated depreciation and record adjustment in government equity account for unrecorded prior year’s depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 17 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2015-008.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following measures to reflect the correct cash in bank balances:

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to request from the depository bank, copies of bank statements in order to verify and determine the nature of those debit memoranda as a basis for the adjustments to arrive at correct cash in bank balance.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the checks issued to avoid accumulation of stale checks. Required adjusting journal entry/ies be prepared immediately for stale checks which are still in the hands of the payee by recognizing accounts payable with corresponding debit to cash in bank. Moreover, replacement of stale checks be done only when the original check is presented for payment.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant to conduct verification of the unbooked bank deposit before preparing adjusting journal entry in order that reconciling items no longer appear in the succeeding bank reconciliation statements.

We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables together with the relevant supporting documents as provided for in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to bring the balance of the inventory account to the correct balance.

We have also recommended that regular conduct of physical inventory count shall be made and periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by concerned office in-charged of inventory management.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited transactions to its appropriate account Trust Liabilities and prepare the journal entry vouchers to adjust the affected account balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office ensure the use of proper accounts in recording the financial transactions of the Municipality pursuant to the revised chart of accounts under the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs in order to achieve fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements and render correct information to Management and other intended users and readers of FS.

We have recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries for the remaining unutilized balance of LGUs share from tobacco excise tax and
The use of appropriate account for transactions being recorded based on accounting rules and regulations issued by competent authorities to avoid misstatements of accounts and ultimately achieve a fairly presented and reliable set of financial statements.

In view of the above-cited observations, we have recommended the following:

For the Municipal Treasurer to automatically separate the collections from building permit fees and other charges into 80% income accruing to the General Fund, 15% held in trust for OBO’s operations and 5% share of DPWH which shall be remitted to the Bureau of Treasury.

For the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to effect the share 15% and 5% share of the OBO and DPWH, respectively from the collection of building permit; and

For the Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare Program of Work detailing the utilization of the 15% share from the collection of building permit, if any and properly observe the guidelines in the preparation of the program of works.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended to the Management the following:

Strengthen its control mechanism to reconcile the records pertaining to the over-remittance and under-remittance of Inter-Agency Payables.

Regularly comply with the law particularly on the remittance of statutory liabilities to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors.

We have recommended that the Municipality direct the officials responsible to demand a refund of the amount received by the remaining 6 ineligible SAP beneficiaries totaling to P33,000.00 and cause the immediate return the unrefunded balance to the DSWD Field Office No. I, San Fernando City, La Union.

To avoid excessive and irregular payment of honoraria to personnel involved in government procurement, we have recommended the following:

That Management ensure that the payment of honorarium of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat must be sourced only from funds specifically authorized and allowed under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

That Management must first exhaust the collections from the sale of bidding documents in payment of BAC honoraria and in case of deficiency, use the agency savings with prior approval of the local Sanggunian.

In case of savings from the local budget, submit a copy of the resolution from the local Sanggunian authorizing the payment of honoraria to BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat chargeable against the General Fund. Non-compliance hereof would warrant issuance of the Notice of Disallowance.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the share of each barangay from the basic real property tax collections within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160 to enable the barangays to augment their funds that is needed to support its projects, programs and activities.

We have recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the prescribed deadline on the remittances of provincial share from basic tax and SEF tax pursuant to the above-cited provisions of law to ensure that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs and activities most beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Pangasinan.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to initiate move by creating Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality to know the existence and condition of these assets and to prepare and submit to the Auditor the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS and inconsonance with Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386.
For the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to complete and maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to show records of the various municipal assets and which will also be used in reconciling with the physical inventory count conducted by the Inventory Committee.

Recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections to the authorized government depository bank daily to protect the government funds from misapplication/improper utilization and refrain from paying expenses out of the collections.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, being the Petty Cash Custodian to comply with the above cited COA Circular and to discontinue  the practice of charging the regular expenses against the Petty Cash Fund.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that management avail of the remedies provided in the Local Government Code with which to collect the tax delinquencies in order to generate more income for the LGU. Further, such action to collect should be within the time prescribed. Otherwise, the LGU shall be estopped from collecting because the action to collect has expired.

Recommendation:
We have recommended for the Local School Board to utilize the Special Education Fund for programs and projects aligned in accordance with the prioritization of expenses as enumerated in DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1 s. 2017 to avoid inappropriate utilization. Furthermore, use of the fund should be maximized in order for the students and teachers to enjoy the benefits that could be derived from implementation of programs and projects as budgeted.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management refrain from the practice of paying and charging prior year expenses to current year appropriation for proper matching of income and expenditures to be able to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. This is in adherence with the above-quoted provisions of R.A. No.7160 and PD 1445.
Expenses of the previous year paid on the current year should be recorded in the books with the following entry:

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management to ensure that all claims are supported with complete documentary requirements to support the validity, propriety and legality of all disbursements.
We have further recommended submission of the approved leave application to support the disbursement pertaining to leave monetization.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to observe correct and proper use of account relative to the expenses incurred especially in the recording of expenses to reflect the accurate amount of OMOE as reported in the Financial Statement.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the paid Disbursement Vouchers including the supporting documents covering the period August, 2017 to December 2017 for GF and SEF as well as October, 2017 to December 2017 for TF.
We have further recommended to require the concerned officials to exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts. This is to avoid undue delay precluding the team from timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports pursuant to COA Circular No.2009-006. Reports when submitted on time are useful information that can aid management in decision making.

We have recommended that all the necessary documents and other requirements be complied by the municipal officials concerned in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001. Further, we recommend that requirements of COA-TAS be complied to ascertain its validity, propriety and legality.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer or her staff to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers/payrolls and its supporting documents after payment is made to prevent the re-use of the same on similar transactions.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the municipality complete the documents/data/information to be posted to completely adhere to the full disclosure policy pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-83 dated August 31, 2010 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 dated September 19, 2011.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should to the fullest extent possible, ensure that all programs/ projects listed in its Annual Investment Program be implemented to give the municipal constituents the maximum benefits that could be derived and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed asset and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to determine responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update PPE ledger cards,and securing of property tags for proper control and identification of these properties.
Continue using Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) and Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and maintain file of these records for easy monitoring and control.
During the exit conference, management told the Audit Team that hopefully next year the LGU will create an item for GSO and eventually will hire the right person to fill-up the position.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections to the authorized government depository bank daily to protect the government funds from misapplication/improper utilization and refrain from paying expenses out of the collections.
The Local Chief Executive assured the Audit Team that this will be implemented since LandBank of the Philippines will soon be opening its branch in Binalonan just across the municipal hall and will be the collecting arm of the LGU.

We have recommended that management require all officials and employees who were granted cash advances to immediately settle and/or liquidate their respective cash advances.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant is advised to monitor closely the timely liquidation of these cash advances and initiate remedial action to enforce immediate settlement especially those cash advances granted on previous years.
The Local Chief Executive ordered the preparation of demand letters to be sent to those officials/employees concerned to immediately settle their cash advances.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the checks issued by the Municipality to avoid stale checks. Moreover, adjusting journal entries be prepared by the Municipal Accountant with respect to stale checks by recognizing Accounts Payable with corresponding debit to Cash in Bank. Likewise, a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher must be furnished to the Municipal Treasurer to effect proper adjustment in the cashbooks.
The Municipal Accountant said that she will make the necessary adjusting journal entry in their records. While the Municipal Treasurer agreed to replace the check upon submission of the stale check.

We have recommended that Management:
To submit Revenue Ordinance enacted by the Sangguniang authorizing the collection of fee from rental/lease of space or real properties owned by the Municipality and its implementing guidelines.
To direct the lessees to pay directly at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office or his deputized collecting agent for appropriate issuance of official receipt for any activity requiring collection of some of money.
To submit the supporting documents showing the computations on the rates of rental and list of merchant-lessees to ascertain the accuracy of the collections.
To formulate written guidelines/policies on the lease or rental of real properties owned by the Municipality, accounting for the collections and the rates and other charges to be applied in connection with the Night Market and Christmas Bazaar and similar events.
The Local Chief Executive noted the audit recommendation.

We have recommended that Management refrain from specifying the brand names in the Purchase Requests pursuant to Section 18 of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to get the widest range of bid proposals.
The Local Chief Executive noted and assured that management will comply with the audit recommendation.

We have recommended to the HRMO to require the submission of duly verified/accepted accomplishment reportsof the job order workers as a means to verify whether or not they have accomplished their assigned task and subsequently assessing the efficiency and effectiveness of the workers.
Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to check on the documentary requirements needed to support the liquidation of payrolls as mentioned on the above provisions to avoid suspension in audit.
The HRMO has already prepared a simplified format of the accomplishment report to be accomplished by the job order workers.

We have recommended that Management should refrain from granting the Cash Gift to Job Order employees pursuant to Section 4 of Budget Circular No. 2016-4 to avoid disallowance in audit.
The Municipal Accountant explained that the reason they released cash gift is that they came across a news item approving the granting of cash gift to job order workers however, no subsequent guidelines were released pertaining to such benefit.

We have recommended the following measures to management:
Require the concerned officials to exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts. This is to avoid undue delay precluding the team from timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations. Reports when submitted on time are useful information that can aid management in decision making, and
Require the offices concerned in the submission of required reports to closely coordinate with each other to facilitate the recording of transactions in the books of accounts and eventual submission of accounts on time.

We have recommended the Local School Board to utilize the Special Education Fund for programs and projects defined under Section 272 of RA 7160 and the implementing guidelines on the utilization of SEF as set under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No.01, 01-A and 01-B, series of 1998, 2000 and 2001 to avoid inappropriate utilization thereof and to maximize the benefit that could be availed under the fund.
Since the municipality had received funds from the National Budget, the Local Chief Executive explained that they did not do more on the schools. He however, noted our recommendation.

We have recommended that management strictly comply with the General  Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.
We have further recommend that adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.
During the exit conference, management expressed compliance with the audit recommendation.

We have recommendedsubmission of certification showing the number of workdays of actual service rendered in a month of an official/ department heads claiming RATA be submitted to serve as basis in computation of actual claims to comply with the abovementioned regulations. The certification should be issued by the Office of HRMO for department heads covered under Section 7.1 of LBC no. 103 and by the Sangguniang Secretary for those officials covered under Section 7.5 of the same Local Budget Circular.
The Sangguniang Bayan Secretary has already submitted certification for the elected officials for the whole calendar year 2016 during the exit conference while HRMO is yet to submit its certification.

We have recommend that utilization of GAD Fund be maximized and that proper planning be made so that all GAD related projects, programs and activities during the year be implemented immediately for the full attainment of its benefits beneficial to their constituents.
The Chairperson-GFPS-TWG of the municipality explained that no one availed of the services provided by the municipality under its GAD Activities and Projects.

We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should endeavor to implement the programs/projects as listed in the Annual Investment Program to the fullest possible to give the townspeople the maximum benefits they could derived and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator gives details for the unexpended amounts but eventually they will be implemented for the coming year.

We have recommend that management adopt measures to attain the intended benefit of the project. Every asset must be utilized to derive results advantageous to the constituents.
We have further recommend that the equipment be safekept in a manner that would preserve its working condition and secured from evil motives such as vandalism or theft.
Management expressed optimism that full implementation of the Livelihood Center will be on 2018.

We have recommended for the management to exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies especially those that have been long overdue for the municipality to have an additional income that could be used to fund various projects and programs that would be beneficial to its constituents of the municipality.
Municipal Treasurer expressed that she will exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies.
